questions.text: Which scientist theoretically predicted the existence of electromagnetic waves?
questions.explanation: James Clerk Maxwell theoretically predicted EM waves by unifying electricity, magnetism, and light.

questions.text: The displacement current arises due to:
questions.explanation: Displacement current is proportional to the rate of change of electric flux (dΦE/dt).

questions.text: The speed of electromagnetic waves in vacuum is given by:
questions.explanation: The speed of light c = 1/√(μ₀ε₀) ≈ 3 × 10⁸ m/s.

questions.text: In an electromagnetic wave, the phase difference between the electric field (E) and magnetic field (B) vectors is:
questions.explanation: The electric and magnetic fields in an EM wave oscillate in phase, reaching maxima and minima at the same time.

questions.text: The direction of propagation of an electromagnetic wave is given by the direction of:
questions.explanation: The Poynting vector, which gives the direction of energy flow, is proportional to E × B.

questions.text: Which of the following has the shortest wavelength in the EM spectrum?
questions.explanation: Gamma rays have the highest frequency and therefore the shortest wavelength.

questions.text: Which waves are used in radar systems for aircraft navigation?
questions.explanation: Microwaves are used in radar due to their short wavelengths, which allow them to be reflected by small objects.

questions.text: Infrared waves are also known as:
questions.explanation: Infrared waves are produced by hot bodies and produce a heating effect upon absorption.

questions.text: The ozone layer in the atmosphere absorbs:
questions.explanation: The ozone layer protects Earth by absorbing harmful UV radiation from the Sun.

questions.text: X-rays are produced by:
options: Bombarding a metal target with high energy electrons
questions.explanation: Sudden deceleration of fast-moving electrons hitting a metal target produces X-rays (Bremsstrahlung).

questions.text: The ratio of the magnitudes of electric field and magnetic field (E/B) in an EM wave is equal to:
questions.explanation: E₀/B₀ = c, where c is the speed of light in that medium.

questions.text: Electromagnetic waves carry:
questions.explanation: EM waves transport both energy and linear momentum through space.

questions.text: Radiation pressure is exerted by EM waves because they:
questions.explanation: When an EM wave is absorbed or reflected by a surface, it transfers momentum, resulting in pressure.

questions.text: The frequency range of visible light is approximately:
questions.explanation: This corresponds to wavelengths from about 400nm to 700nm.

questions.text: Microwave ovens work on the principle of:
questions.explanation: The frequency of microwaves is matched to the resonant frequency of water molecules, causing them to vibrate and heat the food.

questions.text: Which EM waves are used to treat muscular strain?
questions.explanation: Infrared lamps are used in physical therapy for their deep heating effect.

questions.text: The energy of an EM wave is divided between E and B fields in the ratio:
questions.explanation: The average energy density of the electric field equals the average energy density of the magnetic field.

questions.text: Welders wear special glass goggles to protect their eyes from:
questions.explanation: Electric arcs produce high amounts of UV radiation, which can damage the eyes.

questions.text: Greenhouse effect is mainly caused by the trapping of ________ by the atmosphere.
questions.explanation: Visible light enters, is absorbed by Earth, and re-emitted as long-wavelength infrared, which is trapped by greenhouse gases.

questions.text: Who first experimentally demonstrated EM waves in a laboratory?
questions.explanation: Heinrich Hertz used a spark-gap transmitter to generate and detect EM waves, confirming Maxwell's theory.

questions.text: The wavelength of Gamma rays is of the order of:
questions.explanation: Gamma rays have the smallest wavelengths, typically less than 10⁻¹¹ meters.

questions.text: Television remote controls usually use:
questions.explanation: Most remote controls use IR LEDs to transmit coded signals to the receiver.

questions.text: The source of electromagnetic waves is:
options: A charge moving with constant velocity
questions.explanation: Only an accelerating or oscillating charge produces a time-varying electric and magnetic field that propagates as a wave.

questions.text: Which part of the EM spectrum is used in 'LASIK' eye surgery?
questions.explanation: UV radiation (Excimer laser) is used for precision cutting of the cornea.

questions.text: Amper-Maxwell law is expressed as:
questions.explanation: This equation includes both conduction current and displacement current.

questions.text: The velocity of EM waves in a medium of refractive index 'n' is:
questions.explanation: Refractive index n = c/v, so v = c/n.

questions.text: Which of the following cannot be polarized?
questions.explanation: Only transverse waves can be polarized. Sound waves in air are longitudinal.

questions.text: The frequency of a wave remains constant when it passes from one medium to another.
questions.explanation: Frequency is a property of the source, whereas wavelength and speed change with the medium.

questions.text: High frequency radio waves (short wave band) are used for long distance communication by reflection from:
questions.explanation: The ionosphere contains free electrons and ions that reflect certain radio frequencies back to Earth.

questions.text: Which scientist showed that light is an electromagnetic wave?
questions.explanation: Maxwell calculated the speed of EM waves and found it matched the experimental speed of light.

questions.text: Cellular phones use which part of the EM spectrum?
questions.explanation: Mobile phones operate in the radio/microwave frequency bands.

questions.text: Which radiation is used in satellite communication?
questions.explanation: Microwaves can penetrate the atmosphere and travel to satellites in orbit.

questions.text: Water purification systems often use bulbs emitting ________ to kill germs.
questions.explanation: UV radiation can destroy the DNA of bacteria and viruses, making it a powerful disinfectant.

questions.text: In an EM wave, if the electric field oscillates in the y-direction and the wave propagates in the z-direction, the magnetic field oscillates in the:
questions.explanation: E, B, and the direction of propagation are mutually perpendicular.

questions.text: The discovery of X-rays was made by:
questions.explanation: W.C. Roentgen discovered X-rays in 1895 while working with cathode rays.

questions.text: Radio waves are produced by:
questions.explanation: Oscillating charges in antennas generate radio waves.

questions.text: Which radiation is emitted during radioactive decay of nuclei?
questions.explanation: Gamma rays are high-energy photons emitted when a nucleus transitions to a lower energy state.

questions.text: Night vision goggles detect:
questions.explanation: They amplify thermal radiation (Infrared) emitted by objects.

questions.text: Total energy density 'u' of an EM wave is:
questions.explanation: It is the sum of the energy densities of the electric and magnetic fields.

questions.text: Electromagnetic waves are ________ in nature.
questions.explanation: Since the field vectors oscillate perpendicular to the direction of propagation, they are transverse waves.
